title: Generating a DTD from a Component's Data Structure
breadcrumbs: Generating a DTD from a Component's Data Structure
text: Generating a DTD from a Component's Data Structure Generating a DTD from the component's structure overwrites all declarations already stored in the DTD. Fields that are not painted on the component are not added to the DTD. To generate a DTD from a component's data structure: In the DTD Editor, choose File >Load Component Structure and select the component. This generates a DTD for an XML stream with a structure corresponding to the component structure. Optionally add other modeled fields using the DTD Wizard. Related Links Uniface-Compliant DTDs A Document Type Definition (DTD) defines rules for the structure of an XML stream. In Uniface, a DTD is a development object that includes DTD definitions and a default mapping between the XML elements defined by the DTD and Uniface component fields and entities. It can be used to define the rules for the structure of an XML stream that is exchanged between components. Examples of DTDs and XML Streams Generating a DTD From a Modeled Entity You can use the DTD Wizard to create DTDs based on modeled entities. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
